Thanks to the quick actions of everyday folks, police were able to find the driver blocks away from the hit and run. He was easy to spot considering that he plowed through a sign, ran off the road, and hit the median with such force that his airbags deployed and his right wheel appeared to disconnect from the axle. Somehow, the suspect managed to avoid any injury, and police were able to finally get to the bottom of what led to the death of an elderly woman, simply out raking leaves in her yard.

The suspect did not test positive for cocaine and, though a small unopened bottle of liquor was found in the passenger seat of his car, he did not have any alcohol in his system. However, once they secured a search warrant to test his blood, investigators found a medley of depressants, sleep aids, and opiates in his system, including fentanyl. During their investigation, officers photographed the scene of the hit and run and the shoes that were left behind, indicating the speed at which the driver struck the victim. The degree to which the windshield was damaged further demonstrated how dangerously fast the driver was speeding through the residential neighborhood. Blood found on the windshield was later tested and found to be a match for the victim. He was charged with third-degree murder, criminal vehicular homicide, and felony possession of a controlled substance. He refused several plea deals, but also did not want a jury trial. Instead, he opted for a bench trial, where just a judge would determine his fate. He was found guilty on all counts and sentenced to 12 and a half years to be served at St. Cloud Correctional Facility. He can expect to serve a little more than eight years of his sentence in prison and the rest of his time on supervised release.
